title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: The unforgettable novel of a childhood in a sleepy Southern town and the crisis of conscience that rocked it.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A classic romance novel that charts the emotional development of the protagonist Elizabeth Bennet.

author: George Orwell
description: Among the seminal texts of the 20th century, 1984 is a rare work that grows more haunting as its futuristic purgatory becomes more real.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
description: The hero-narrator of The Catcher in the Rye is an ancient child of sixteen, a native New Yorker named Holden Caulfield.

title: The Lord of the Rings
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: One Ring to rule them all, One Ring to find them, One Ring to bring them all and in the darkness bind them.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
description: Harry Potter has no idea how famous he is. That's because he's being raised by his miserable aunt and uncle.

title: Animal Farm
author: George Orwell
description: A farm is taken over by its overworked, mistreated animals. With flaming idealism and stirring slogans, they set out to create a paradise of progress, justice, and equality.

title: The Diary of a Young Girl
author: Anne Frank
description: Discovered in the attic in which she spent the last years of her life, Anne Frank's remarkable diary has since become a world classic.

title: The Little Prince
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry
description: A pilot crashes in the Sahara Desert and meets a young prince fallen from an asteroid.

title: The Chronicles of Narnia
author: C.S. Lewis
description: Journeys to the end of the world, fantastic creatures, and epic battles between good and evil.

title: Fahrenheit 451
author: Ray Bradbury
description: Guy Montag is a fireman. His job is to destroy the most illegal of commodities, the printed book, along with the houses in which they are hidden.

title: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Brontë
description: A novel of intense power and intrigue, Jane Eyre has dazzled generations of readers with its depiction of a woman's quest for freedom.

title: Wuthering Heights
author: Emily Brontë
description: The passionate and destructive love between Heathcliff and Catherine Earnshaw on the Yorkshire moors.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: A profoundly important classic of world literature, Brave New World is a searching vision of an unequal, technologically-advanced future.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A great modern classic and the prelude to The Lord of the Rings.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A masterly psychological study of a man whose mind is torn apart by guilt and paranoia after he commits a murder.

title: The Odyssey
author: Homer
description: The epic tale of Odysseus and his ten-year journey home after the Trojan War.

title: The Brothers Karamazov
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A passionate philosophical novel set in 19th-century Russia, that enters deeply into the ethical debates of God, free will, and morality.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
description: The epic saga of Captain Ahab's obsessive quest for the white whale, Moby Dick.

title: War and Peace
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: A panoramic study of early 19th-century Russian society, noted for its mastery of realistic detail and variety of psychological analysis.
